description: ol-too-many-inputs (issue #111): `first` is a strictly fixed-arity-1 Core primitive with no variadic form. The reader caps a bare call at that arity, so the only way to over-supply a primitive is the parenthesized form — `(first 1 2)` explicitly groups two inputs. Because `first` has no alternate/variadic paren form (corePrimitiveArityRange gives max === min === 1), this raises too-many outside a variadic form (spec/tooling.md:182), pointing at the callee. Contrast arity-paren-variadic-ok, where `(print …)`/`(random a b)` do have such forms and are left alone.
